tiktok.caption: $611/mo lease vs $738/mo to buy. Sounds like a no-brainer — until you see what 3 leases actually costs you.

youtube_short.title: The Car Lease Trap That Costs $22,000
title_alternatives: Stop Leasing — Here's the Real Math
youtube_short.description: The dealer shows you $611/month to lease vs $738/month to buy — and your brain always picks the lower number. But after 36 months you've paid $21,996 and walk away with zero equity. Three leases over 9 years? That's $66,000 paid and nothing to show for it. Then add mileage overage fees at $0.30/mile — the average American drives 15K/year and pays an extra $900 just for going about their life. The smarter play: buy reliable, drive it to 150K miles, invest the difference — and come out $22,000 ahead over a decade. #carlease #personalfinance #moneytips #leasevsbuy Follow for more finance math they hope you never see.

hook_line: The lease saves you $127/month. It costs you $22,000 over a decade.
